name: Header
purpose: Contains the date, delivery method, and the recipient's name, title, organization, and address.

name: Subject Line
purpose: The 'Re:' line indicating the subject matter of the letter and the claims being made.

name: Salutation
purpose: The formal opening greeting to the recipient.

name: Introduction
purpose: Statement of legal representation, background on the clients, and an overview of the claims being made.

name: Statement of Facts
purpose: Detailed chronological account of the incidents, grievances, and discriminatory or unlawful conduct.

name: Legal Basis and Violations
purpose: Explanation of how the opposing party's conduct violates specific state or federal laws.

name: Conclusion and Demand
purpose: Statement of damages, emotional distress, threat of imminent litigation, and an offer to resolve the matter amicably out of court.

name: Preservation of Evidence Notice
purpose: Formal demand to preserve all relevant physical and electronically stored information, devices, and communications (spoliation hold).

name: Response Deadline
purpose: Specific deadline given to the recipient to respond to discuss an amicable resolution before a lawsuit is filed.

name: Sign-off and Signature
purpose: The formal closing valediction and the name and firm of the attorney sending the letter.
